0.response: {"Q0":"I would decide if I liked the top or bottom galaxy better initially and say \"top\" or \"bottom\" to make me remember, and then just look for which arrow went with that choice when they were shown again. On the planets that had gems that could be sold, I dug every time no matter what the rock probability there was; not sure if that was a good choice or not. Both the blue and red planets' digging sequences ended the same so I only had to remember which way to start depending where I was. Once the red planet astronaut left, I tried to get all the blue gens and then whatever ice covered ones I could in the asteroid field. if the values for the galaxies were similar but the probabilities very different, I'd usually go with the higher probability.  I'd almost always go with the higher probability when one was really high and the other was low even if the amounts were very different.  When both probabilities were low, I figured I might as well just try for the larger amount. If both probabilities were enough over 50% I would usually go with the higher amount even though it was a bit lower."}

7.response: {"Q0":"If the values for the galaxies were similar but the probabilities very different, I'd usually go with the higher probability.  I'd almost always go with the higher probability when one was really high and the other was low even if the amounts were very different.  When both probabilities were low, I figured I might as well just try for the larger amount. If both probabilities were enough over 50% I would usually go with the higher amount even though it was a bit lower."}

1.response: {"Q0":"The only time I think I really changed anything was when the red astronaut left.  Then I started treating the red planet like the yellow one and only had to remember the blue starting digging tool. I no longer tried to get any red gems in the asteroid field."}

2.response: {"Q0":"I am not even sure but it seemed pretty low for both.  I'd say you night have gotten a gem < 40% of the time on both. If there was a  difference between the 2 I didn't notice it.  It didn;t seem enough to have to choose when to dig to me."}

8.response: {"Q0":"I had to focus most on choosing galaxies to make sure I chose in time. There was much more time to dig than there was to hit the correct arrow.  I had to keep telling myself which one I preferred and then just look for the arrow that went with that choice. I also kept starting the red sequence like the blue at first and had to make sure to get that right until it was easy."}

10.response: {"Q0":"well when the times got shorter, it sounded like we would be doing just the planets, but it was the whole thing!!!"}

11.response: {"Q0":"I liked it! I guess shorter planet times and longer galaxy ones when mixed with planets would be good though. "}
